structure_blocks.heading: Contract approval and signing authorities
structure_blocks.text: Approval authority limits for PSPC buyers are subject to the following conditions:

structure_blocks.heading: Contract approval and signing authorities
structure_blocks.text: for requirements or amendments requiring approval, buyers must ensure the terms and conditions of the contract are consistent with the representations made to them as to the substantive nature of the transaction.

structure_blocks.heading: Contract approval and signing authorities
structure_blocks.text: Legal Services must be consulted when the buyer is considering a deviation from standard contracting clauses, standard templates, or legally approved commodity or sector-based templates. Buyers may also consult Legal Services on sensitive requirements.

structure_blocks.heading: Contract approval and signing authorities
structure_blocks.text: Conditions and limits imposed on the approval authority limits for PSPC buyers will apply to the approval and signing of all contractual documents and arrangements. This includes arrangements that are not subject to the Government Contracts Regulations and the TB Directive on the Management of Procurement . The exception is if approval and signing limits are set out within an existing memorandum of understanding.

structure_blocks.heading: Contract approval and signing authorities
structure_blocks.text: The Deputy Minister's approval is required when the value of transfer agreements between departments (for example, transfer orders with CORCAN) exceeds the approval limits of PSPC Acquisitions Program (AP).

structure_blocks.heading: Contract approval and signing authorities
structure_blocks.text: TB approval is required when the value of contractual arrangements with provincial or municipal governments, or provincial or federal Crown corporations exceeds PSPC AP approval limits.

structure_blocks.heading: Contract approval and signing authorities
structure_blocks.text: Approval of contracts that include options must be sought in accordance with the total estimated cost including any options for which funds are available or expected to be provided in the future.

structure_blocks.heading: Contract approval and signing authorities
structure_blocks.text: More than one contractual document must not be issued, under any circumstance, in order to circumvent the necessity of obtaining the proper approval authority

structure_blocks.heading: Contract approval and signing authorities
structure_blocks.text: If an Advance Contract Award Notice (ACAN) is published directing a requirement to a specific business for the purposes of contract approval authority, it is classified under the “competitive” category. Competitive approval authority levels apply, whenever an ACAN has been posted and no valid statement of capabilities was submitted.

structure_blocks.heading: Contract approval and signing authorities
structure_blocks.text: Exceptional contracting limits permit PSPC to enter into:

structure_blocks.heading: Contract approval and signing authorities
structure_blocks.text: any form of agreement used by a railway company for permission to construct or maintain a private crossing, a pipe or cable crossing over or under the property of the company at a rate or in an amount no greater than those normally charged for each permission

structure_blocks.heading: Contract approval and signing authorities
structure_blocks.text: any agreement with a railway, telegraph, telephone or power company for permission to attach wire to poles belonging to the company at a rate or in an amount no greater than those normally charged for each permission

structure_blocks.heading: Contract approval and signing authorities
structure_blocks.text: amend contracts with the United States government containing that government's usual terms dealing with indemnity and liability, subject to the limits of TB Directive on the Management of Procurement , Appendix A (Contracting Approvals)

raw_text:
Contract approval and signing authorities

Buyers are delegated authority to enter into contracts and to sign and amend contracts in accordance with the level of responsibility of the position they occupy.

Contract approval and signing authorities in support of clients’ programs must be done in accordance with the relevant legislation and regulations, and within PSPC policies and guidelines.

These authorities apply to all contractual documents and arrangements, including:

purchase orders

contracts

formal agreements and arrangements (for example, an interdepartmental memorandum of understanding)

standing offers and supply arrangements

letters of intent

go-ahead letters and go-ahead messages

stores transfer orders

supply transfer orders

written direction to the agency of record (for example, a work authorization)

assignments

consents to subcontract

termination for convenience notices

amendments to any of the above

Approval authority limits for PSPC buyers are subject to the following conditions:

for requirements or amendments requiring approval, buyers must ensure the terms and conditions of the contract are consistent with the representations made to them as to the substantive nature of the transaction.

Legal Services must be consulted when the buyer is considering a deviation from standard contracting clauses, standard templates, or legally approved commodity or sector-based templates. Buyers may also consult Legal Services on sensitive requirements.

Conditions and limits imposed on the approval authority limits for PSPC buyers will apply to the approval and signing of all contractual documents and arrangements. This includes arrangements that are not subject to the Government Contracts Regulations and the TB Directive on the Management of Procurement . The exception is if approval and signing limits are set out within an existing memorandum of understanding.

The Deputy Minister's approval is required when the value of transfer agreements between departments (for example, transfer orders with CORCAN) exceeds the approval limits of PSPC Acquisitions Program (AP).

TB approval is required when the value of contractual arrangements with provincial or municipal governments, or provincial or federal Crown corporations exceeds PSPC AP approval limits.

Approval of contracts that include options must be sought in accordance with the total estimated cost including any options for which funds are available or expected to be provided in the future.

More than one contractual document must not be issued, under any circumstance, in order to circumvent the necessity of obtaining the proper approval authority

If an Advance Contract Award Notice (ACAN) is published directing a requirement to a specific business for the purposes of contract approval authority, it is classified under the “competitive” category. Competitive approval authority levels apply, whenever an ACAN has been posted and no valid statement of capabilities was submitted.

Exceptional contracting limits permit PSPC to enter into:

any form of agreement used by a railway company for permission to construct or maintain a private crossing, a pipe or cable crossing over or under the property of the company at a rate or in an amount no greater than those normally charged for each permission

any agreement with a railway, telegraph, telephone or power company for permission to attach wire to poles belonging to the company at a rate or in an amount no greater than those normally charged for each permission

amend contracts with the United States government containing that government's usual terms dealing with indemnity and liability, subject to the limits of TB Directive on the Management of Procurement , Appendix A (Contracting Approvals)
markdown_text:
Contract approval and signing authorities

Buyers are delegated authority to enter into contracts and to sign and amend contracts in accordance with the level of responsibility of the position they occupy.

Contract approval and signing authorities in support of clients’ programs must be done in accordance with the relevant legislation and regulations, and within PSPC policies and guidelines.

These authorities apply to all contractual documents and arrangements, including:

purchase orders

contracts

formal agreements and arrangements (for example, an interdepartmental memorandum of understanding)

standing offers and supply arrangements

letters of intent

go-ahead letters and go-ahead messages

stores transfer orders

supply transfer orders

written direction to the agency of record (for example, a work authorization)

assignments

consents to subcontract

termination for convenience notices

amendments to any of the above

Approval authority limits for PSPC buyers are subject to the following conditions:

for requirements or amendments requiring approval, buyers must ensure the terms and conditions of the contract are consistent with the representations made to them as to the substantive nature of the transaction.

Legal Services must be consulted when the buyer is considering a deviation from standard contracting clauses, standard templates, or legally approved commodity or sector-based templates. Buyers may also consult Legal Services on sensitive requirements.

Conditions and limits imposed on the approval authority limits for PSPC buyers will apply to the approval and signing of all contractual documents and arrangements. This includes arrangements that are not subject to the Government Contracts Regulations and the TB Directive on the Management of Procurement . The exception is if approval and signing limits are set out within an existing memorandum of understanding.

The Deputy Minister's approval is required when the value of transfer agreements between departments (for example, transfer orders with CORCAN) exceeds the approval limits of PSPC Acquisitions Program (AP).

TB approval is required when the value of contractual arrangements with provincial or municipal governments, or provincial or federal Crown corporations exceeds PSPC AP approval limits.

Approval of contracts that include options must be sought in accordance with the total estimated cost including any options for which funds are available or expected to be provided in the future.

More than one contractual document must not be issued, under any circumstance, in order to circumvent the necessity of obtaining the proper approval authority

If an Advance Contract Award Notice (ACAN) is published directing a requirement to a specific business for the purposes of contract approval authority, it is classified under the “competitive” category. Competitive approval authority levels apply, whenever an ACAN has been posted and no valid statement of capabilities was submitted.

Exceptional contracting limits permit PSPC to enter into:

any form of agreement used by a railway company for permission to construct or maintain a private crossing, a pipe or cable crossing over or under the property of the company at a rate or in an amount no greater than those normally charged for each permission

any agreement with a railway, telegraph, telephone or power company for permission to attach wire to poles belonging to the company at a rate or in an amount no greater than those normally charged for each permission

amend contracts with the United States government containing that government's usual terms dealing with indemnity and liability, subject to the limits of TB Directive on the Management of Procurement , Appendix A (Contracting Approvals)
